rulings.Case Title: T&C PROPERTIES, LLC VS. ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Respondent Tandc Properties Llcs Second Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs In The Post Arbitration Judicial Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 17, 2023 line 1. T&C PROPERTIES' SECOND AMENDED MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES IN THE POST ARBITRATION JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS is DENIED. T&C is not the prevailing party in the litigation phase (action filed November 19, 2020) of the dispute and therefore is not entitled to attorney's fees or costs for work done in relation to the litigation. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: T&C PROPERTIES, LLC VS. ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 17, 2023 line 1. ST. JAMES' MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES is GRANTED as to fees and costs associated with the litigation phase of the dispute since it is the prevailing party. Those fees and costs amount to $318,715.65. The ruling regarding St. James' fees and costs associated with the arbitration phase of the dispute is dependent on the parties' stipulation. If the parties stipulate to the Court's authority to determine the amount of fees related to the litigation and arbitration, the Court GRANTS St. James motion and awards it $438,623.5 in fees and $55,974.91 in costs.. This award includes both arbitration-related fees and costs and litigation-related fees and costs. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RODRIGO LOPEZ VS. ALTAS PROPERTY GROUP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment To Clearly Identify Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 17, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO AMEND JUDGMENT TO CLEARLY IDENTIFY COSTS is GRANTED in part. On September 9, 2021 the Court executed a form of judgment submitted by Defendants' counsel of record. The form of judgment prepared by the defendants did not include a determination that defendants are the prevailing party or that the defendants are to recover costs. At least one of these finding are necessary to alert the clerks to applicability of CRC 3.1700(b)(4) for the purposes of entry of costs on the judgment entered in favor of a prevailing party whose Memorandum of Costs matured. The clerk's duties are purely ministerial and are based on strict compliance with procedure and the Court's orders. The form of judgment having not given any indication that the costs have been awarded (explicitly) or could be awarded to the prevailing party (CCP 1032), was incomplete. Therefore, the judgment is amended nunc pro tunc to include the following language: "Defendants to recover costs per memorandum of costs." (For future reference and guidance see form JUD-100 6(b)). Defendants to present an order in conformity with this ruling and a form of amended judgment. Defendants thereafter may apply for an Abstract of Judgment. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ROSEMARIE BENTER VS. LAURA FERRIS BICHE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 17, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. The First Amended Complaint does not sufficiently state a cause of action against Defendant Eugene Gardner for (1) quiet title, because it does not sufficiently plead that the loan is void or that the purchase was improper, (2) financial elder abuse, because it does not sufficiently plead that Defendant Gardner's use of the Property was wrongful, (3) declaratory relief, because it does not sufficiently plead prospective relief. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: URBAN PACIFIC PROPERTIES, INC. VS. CECILE RICHARDS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion To Quash Depostion Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Motion For Protective Order; Declaration Of Hadley Rood
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 17, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSTION SUBPOENAS FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS AND MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER is DENIED. The Court finds (1) the requested documents are relevant and (2) the Defendants' right to privacy in unspecified financial documents is not outweighed by the Plaintiff's competing interest in obtaining discoverable evidence. Defendants' Motion for Protective Order is DENIED. Defendants did not specify which documents should be protected. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO BY/THR SAN VS. HERRINGBONE TAVERN, INC. DBA FISHERMAN'S GROTTO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 17, 2023 line 8. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Plaintiff sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer under California Code of Civil Procedure, subsection 1161(2). The Court finds the lease does not require thirty (30) days' notice for nonpayment of rent. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC VS. THOMAS WEEMS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 17, 2023 line 10. DEFENDANT THOMAS WEEMS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. California Code of Civil Procedure section 1161(2) does not require 3-Day Notices to Pay or Quit to specifically state that Saturdays, Sundays, and judicial holidays are excluded from the three days' notice. Further, the Complaint sufficiently states two causes of action; one cause of action is based on the 3-Day Notice to Pay or Quit; the other cause of action is based on the 3-Day Notice to Quit. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
